mechanism_narrative: Lower unemployment rates indicate a healthier labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE level vs 20-day forward returns is not negative at a statistically significant level over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below a low threshold, it signals highly accommodative monetary policy, compressing discount rates and boosting equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: In instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad-based demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > its 3-month moving average and RSAFS > its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return after both conditions are met is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC of joint indicator is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, further increases in consumer prices erode purchasing power and raise uncertainty, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Within periods where CPI YoY > 3%, a further rise in CPIAUCSL does not produce a statistically significant negative 20-day forward return (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal an impending contraction in economic activity, which depresses corporate earnings and stock prices.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Following the trigger condition, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A better-than-expected payrolls report surprises markets upward, reinforcing optimism about labor market recovery and driving short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return after positive payroll surprises is not significantly greater than zero (p > 0.10) or is lower than the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: In the two years following the trough of the Great Recession, rising real GDP signals a durable recovery, increasing investor confidence and stock valuations.
failure_condition: From 2010 to 2012, the correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward 60-day returns is not positive or fails to be statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A strong year-over-year increase in retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and supports positive stock returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Following the trigger, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate is below 55%.
